title: Canadian Effective Exchange Rates
summary: This is a methodology and data reference page from the Bank of Canada describing the Canadian Effective Exchange Rate (CEER) index, which replaced the CERI in January 2018. The page explains the index composition (17 currencies), calculation methodology, and weight structure for tracking Canadian dollar competitiveness against major trading partners.
key_points: CEER index includes 17 foreign currencies from countries accounting for at least 0.5% of Canadian non-oil exports (>93% coverage) and imports (>91% coverage)
The major currencies index includes 7 currencies: US dollar, euro, Japanese yen, British pound, Swiss franc, Australian dollar, and Swedish krona

The OITP index includes 10 currencies: Brazilian real, Chinese renminbi, Hong Kong dollar, Indian rupee, Malaysian ringgit, Mexican peso, Norwegian krone, Peruvian new sol, South Korean won, and Thai baht
Energy trade is excluded from weight calculations to be consistent with international standards and minimize volatility from oil price swings
Trade weights are updated annually and based on import weights, export weights, and third-market competition weights
Real CEER indices adjust nominal indices for CPI price changes and are available only monthly
why_it_matters: This page is a reference resource explaining how Canada's exchange rate is measured against trading partners. It does not contain market-moving information or current data—it's encyclopedic content about methodology. The index is useful for assessing Canadian dollar competitiveness in international trade and distinguishing between financial pressures (major currencies) and competitiveness issues (OITP).
macro_context: The CEER index provides a comprehensive measure of Canadian dollar value weighted by trade patterns. The OITP index captures competitiveness-related dynamics with emerging markets while the major currencies index reflects financial pressure dynamics. Energy exclusion prevents oil price volatility from distorting trade competitiveness measures.
